title: 1 million people see YOU ARE NOT ALONE in Japan
display_title: 1 million people see YOU ARE NOT ALONE in Japan
wikitext:
[]: 1 million people see YOU ARE (NOT) ALONE in Japan             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Fri Sep 28, 2007 3:20 pm says ANN I'm not familiar with the Japanese box office, but I'm guessing that's pretty impressive, especially for an anime. RoE ftw!

[]:             Quote Post by marmotgirl » Fri Sep 28, 2007 4:22 pm One million people see YA(N)A...and yet *I* do not. This situation is unacceptable.

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Fri Sep 28, 2007 6:23 pm marmotgirl wrote: One million people see YA(N)A...and yet *I* do not. This situation is unacceptable.

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Fri Sep 28, 2007 7:13 pm Quick, let's kidnap Anno and force him to give us a free showing with his own director's commentary!

[]:             Quote Post by NAveryW » Fri Sep 28, 2007 11:41 pm ANN wrote: The blog entry then encourages readers to see the movie a second or third time to make "new discoveries about Eva."

[]:             Quote Post by Sailor Star Dust » Sat Sep 29, 2007 2:08 am Brik-kun wrote: Quick, let's kidnap Anno and force him to give us a free showing with his own director's commentary!

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Sat Sep 29, 2007 6:18 am Sailor Star Dust wrote: With Rebuild having 1 million people see it, I think I heard that recently. Apparently it also broke some box office record, which is just freaking awesome.

[]:             Quote Post by Lleonard Pler » Sun Sep 30, 2007 2:23 am I was one of them! I watched the movie 15 days ago, during my trip to Japan, in a cinema of Shinjuku (Tokyo). The cinema was full of people. :P These figures are impressive for a country where, it's said, people don't go to cinema.

[]:             Quote Post by Tokpile Quohog » Sun Sep 30, 2007 2:32 am Lleonard Pler wrote: I was one of them! I watched the movie 15 days ago, during my trip to Japan, in a cinema of Shinjuku (Tokyo). The cinema was full of people. :P These figures are impressive for a country where, it's said, people don't go to cinema.

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Sun Sep 30, 2007 7:38 am Lleonard Pler wrote: I was one of them! I watched the movie 15 days ago, during my trip to Japan, in a cinema of Shinjuku (Tokyo). The cinema was full of people. :P These figures are impressive for a country where, it's said, people don't go to cinema.

[]:             Quote Post by Lleonard Pler » Sun Sep 30, 2007 9:15 am In the cinema, only the movie book was on sale, and almost everyone was buying it, it sold for 800 yens. Nothing spectacular, but a nice souvenir. The manga-anime shops were full of merchandising and comics, though. Rei was the cover of important magazines like Newtype and Rolling Stone (!).

[]:             Quote Post by CitizenGeek » Wed Oct 03, 2007 1:20 pm And now it appears that RoE has reached another milstone - According to ANN YOU ARE (NOT) ALONE is the top grossing Eva movie. It beats EoE by a fairly narrow margin. Also, this article answers our questions on how EoE done. It made 1.45 billion yen (US$12.6 million) which is a lot of money, but not quite up there with the Hollywood blockbusters, I would think!

[]:             Quote Post by the_seventh_child » Wed Oct 03, 2007 1:35 pm marmotgirl wrote: One million people see YA(N)A...

[]:             Quote Post by TriLink » Wed Oct 03, 2007 4:34 pm BrikHaus wrote: Quick, let's kidnap Anno

[]:             Quote Post by slothen » Wed Oct 03, 2007 4:47 pm What? Kidnapping of creative Japanese masterminds? made me think of this: http://www.penny-arcade.com/comic/2000/04/12 http://www.penny-arcade.com/comic/2000/04/14 And just think how much more money they have to make with 3 more movies coming out.

[]:             Quote Post by Gendo'sPapa » Wed Oct 03, 2007 11:28 pm Has an announcement been made that there's actually three more Eva movies coming out? Or is it still that goofy and falsely advertised- 3.0 and FINAL playing back to back in one film? I'd love for a R'Eva Quartet. Not A Quartet in name only.

[]:             Quote Post by Avatar of Dreams » Thu Oct 04, 2007 11:01 pm When I read this thread, it brought a smile to my face to see Rebuild's success. ...Then I was reminded that I am not one of these one million lucky people. Ahhhhh! I support kidnapping Anno! :twisted:

[]:             Quote Post by BrikHaus » Thu Oct 04, 2007 11:10 pm Gendo'sPapa wrote: Or is it still that goofy and falsely advertised- 3.0 and FINAL playing back to back in one film?

[]: omg             Quote Post by iloveallecchianime » Sun Oct 21, 2007 2:56 am I better have a heart attack when I see the movie from it being so good or im gonna fly to Japan and slap Anno for making me wait 2 months WITHOUT EVEN A RAW!!!!!

[]:             Quote Post by CtHuLhU » Sun Oct 21, 2007 8:49 am 2 million people have looked YOU ARE (NOT) ALONE in Japan
